reasoning: Iteration 1 regressed (15.41ms → 20.38ms) due to zone map overhead. However, the hot scan loop remains scalar despite AVX512 availability. SIMD vectorization has 4-6x speedup potential (20ms → 3-5ms), which would beat both GenDB baseline (12.84ms) and DuckDB (20.96ms). With only 1 iteration left, we must be aggressive. The correctness issue is a storage-level data loss bug (62 missing rows), not fixable by query optimization. Focus on CPU-bound SIMD optimization to recover from regression and achieve breakthrough performance.
focus_areas: SIMD vectorization for filter predicates (date, discount, quantity) using AVX512 intrinsics
Branch-free filter evaluation with selection vectors to eliminate pipeline stalls
strategy_notes: Be aggressive with SIMD implementation. Target 3-5ms execution time (4-6x speedup). The system has rollback capability, so failed optimization only costs iteration budget. This is our last chance to achieve breakthrough performance. Combine vectorized filters with masked aggregation for maximum impact. If successful, we'll significantly outperform both baseline and DuckDB.
notes: Execution Optimizer agent should implement AVX512 SIMD vectorization in the scan loop (lines 228-244 from iter_1). Process 8-16 rows per instruction for date/discount/quantity filters. Use masked operations for conditional aggregation. Fallback to AVX2 if needed. Include tail handling for remainder rows. Target: 3-5ms execution time.
